"""
FORGE — Human Performance OS · COMPLETE iOS SOURCE (single file)
================================================================================
This one file contains the entire native SwiftUI iOS app: 116 files
(Swift sources, the Xcode project + shared scheme, entitlements, privacy
manifest, home-screen widget extension, workout Live Activity, Apple Watch companion app, app icon, tests).

It is self-extracting. Anyone with a Mac can rebuild the real project:

  1. Save this file (e.g. Forge-iOS-Source-Bundle.txt).
  2. In Terminal:  python3 Forge-iOS-Source-Bundle.txt
     -> recreates the ./Forge/ project folder beside this file (binary assets too).
  3. Open Forge/Forge.xcodeproj in Xcode 16+, pick an iPhone simulator, Run.
     Tests: press Cmd-U.  CLI: xcodebuild -scheme Forge ... build test

Verified green on CI (macOS, Xcode 16): the app compiles and all 143 unit tests
pass. See .github/workflows/ci.yml and github.com/seanamecum/forge/actions.

No Python? Every line after the BEGIN marker is prefixed with "#": strip that
one character, and text files sit between ">>>>FORGE_FILE>>>> <path>" markers
(the app icon is base64 after ">>>>FORGE_FILE64>>>> <path>").

Stack: SwiftUI · MVVM · @Observable · SwiftData · HealthKit · WidgetKit ·
Swift Charts · Claude Messages API (backend proxy or direct; offline mock
fallback) · unified multi-wearable data layer · local notifications.
Bundle ID com.forge.performance, iOS 17.0, demo athlete "Sean Calloway".
AI: point CoachProxyURL at the deployed supabase coach-proxy function (or set
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY locally for dev); with neither, the coach runs offline.
Non-technical partners: open Forge-Demo.html instead -- clickable product demo.
================================================================================
"""
# Self-extractor. Run:  python3 <thisfile>
import os, sys, io, base64
SELF = sys.argv[0]
TXT = ">>>>FORGE_FILE>>>> "
B64 = ">>>>FORGE_FILE64>>>> "
NL = chr(10)
with io.open(SELF, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    lines = f.read().split(NL)
start = lines.index("# ===FORGE-BUNDLE-BEGIN===") + 1
path = None; mode = None; chunk = []; count = 0
def flush():
    global count
    if path is None:
        return
    if chunk and chunk[-1] == "":
        chunk.pop()          # writer guarantees one trailing newline per file
    d = os.path.dirname(path)
    if d:
        os.makedirs(d, exist_ok=True)
    if mode == "txt":
        with io.open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as out:
            out.write(NL.join(chunk) + NL)
    else:
        with open(path, "wb") as out:
            out.write(base64.b64decode("".join(chunk)))
    count += 1
for raw in lines[start:]:
    if not raw.startswith("#"):
        continue             # tolerate stray blank lines at EOF
    line = raw[1:]           # payload lines carry a one-character comment prefix
    if line.startswith(TXT) or line.startswith(B64):
        flush()
        mode = "txt" if line.startswith(TXT) else "b64"
        path = line[len(TXT):] if mode == "txt" else line[len(B64):]
        chunk = []
    else:
        chunk.append(line)
flush()
print("Extracted %d files -> ./Forge/" % count)
sys.exit(0)
